summary: With only 2 results and E001 confirming OI-crowding continuation as viable, the largest structural gaps are: (1) funding×premium joint signals unexploited, (2) smart-money vs crowd divergence via top_trader ratios, (3) realized-vol regime gating absent from filter set, (4) basis normalization as an exit family, and (5) cross-channel conflict suppression in sub-2m windows.

proposals.notes: Enter short when premium_bps spikes >2σ AND funding is already positive AND OI is rising — triple crowding confirmation that historically precedes basis mean-reversion.
proposals.rationale: Existing OI entries use oi_change_pct alone; no archetype combines premium_bps + funding + OI in a joint threshold to identify perp-overheating before forced unwind.

proposals.notes: Enter long when top_trader_position_ratio is rising (smart-money net long) while global_long_short_ratio is falling (retail net short) and OI is expanding — fade the crowd while following informed flow.
proposals.rationale: No existing archetype exploits the divergence between top_trader_position_ratio and global_long_short_ratio as an entry signal; this captures informed-vs-uninformed flow split unavailable from OI or price alone.

proposals.notes: Enter short when taker_buy_ratio_5m >0.7 in a thin liquidity_bucket during low realized_vol_30m — aggressive retail buying in illiquid low-vol conditions tends to exhaust quickly and reverse.
proposals.rationale: Existing pricechange entries do not condition on liquidity_bucket × taker imbalance × vol regime simultaneously; this three-way conjunction targets a specific microstructure overextension pattern.

proposals.notes: Exit when basis reverts within 1σ of its 30m mean after entry was triggered by a premium/OI spike — captures the mean-reversion completion point rather than a fixed TP/SL.
proposals.rationale: All 100 existing exits use fixed or ATR-based TP/SL or trailing stops; no exit archetype uses basis or premium_bps normalization as the primary exit trigger, which is a distinct exit family.

proposals.notes: Pass only signals where realized_vol_30m is in the top tercile AND expanding versus the prior 30m window — ensures entries land in breakout regimes where momentum strategies historically outperform mean-reversion.
proposals.rationale: Existing 120 filters cover session, weekday, btc_24h_pct, and macro events but no archetype gates on realized_vol_30m regime direction (expanding vs contracting), which is a fundamentally different risk-regime dimension.

proposals.notes: Pass only signals on assets with btc_correlation_30d below the universe median — idiosyncratic BWE-driven alpha is highest when an asset is decorrelated from BTC macro flow.
proposals.rationale: btc_correlation_30d appears in the available feature set but no existing filter archetype uses it as a gate; combined with market_cap_bucket it creates a novel 2D universe-selection filter distinct from all existing channel or session filters.

proposals.notes: Suppress entry when two channels fire opposing signals (one bullish, one bearish) within a 90-second window AND premium_bps spread between them exceeds 1σ — conflicting fast signals in thin premium windows indicate noise, not conviction.
proposals.rationale: Existing 60 cross-channel archetypes combine correlated channel signals for confluence; none explicitly detect and suppress on directional conflict within sub-2m windows, which is a structurally opposite logic family.

proposals.notes: Enter long when burst_count_* exceeds its 30m rolling 80th percentile AND oi_change_5m is positive AND pretrend is already bullish — repeated message bursts co-occurring with OI accumulation signal sustained institutional interest, not one-off noise.
proposals.rationale: Reserved6 has 35 existing entries; none use burst_count_* as a primary signal — burst frequency as a message-activity proxy for institutional accumulation is an unexploited signal dimension for this channel.
